index fc92663..4019691 100644
--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -506,2 +506,17 @@
   - Ads remain off. Capacity remains tier 6.
 - Watch next week: whether A tier 2 meaningfully reduces compute, whether S2 price/promo cuts slow churn, whether issue age responds, and whether any enterprise thread finally closes.
+
+## Week 29 / Day 196
+
+- Cash: $2,432,014. Individual subscribers fell to 89,307 after 28,286 cancellations. New subscribers only 1. Enterprise still has zero subscribed seats.
+- Week 28 cash flow was unsustainable: subscription payments only $271,781; ops -$1,638,000; compute -$255,225; capacity -$196,000. A tier 2 reduced compute, but the support-heavy retention bet failed.
+- Active book: S2 A 88,121, S2 B 738, S2 C 3. This is now a shrinking cash-harvest business.
+- S2 issues: 20,514 open, avg age 7.21 days, max 139. Support spend did not prevent issue growth.
+- Responded to 197 fresh enterprise leads only: D_E02 173, D_E08 23, D_E09 1.
+- Changes:
+  - Survival reset: cut operations to $36.5k/day total (global $10k + targeted $26.5k).
+  - Removed S2/S3 ongoing promotions; discounts did not slow churn enough to justify the cash loss.
+  - Kept development and ads at $0.
+  - Moved capacity from tier 6 to tier 5 to save $126k/week. Current usage should fit unless enterprise finally closes.
+  - Kept A2/B3/C5, prices $89/$249/$449, quotas A60000/B200000/C500000.
+- Watch next week: cash runway after cost reset, whether capacity tier 5 stays safe, whether enterprise finally closes, and remaining S2 payment volume.
